tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: Congratulations, Pakistan. You're Finally No. 1. Not in education. Not in the economy. Not in innovation. According to the Global Terrorism Index 2026, Pakistan is now ranked as the world's most terrorism affected country, overtaking Burkina Faso. The report states that nearly 20% of all terrorism related deaths worldwide occurred in Pakistan. In other words, around one in every five people killed in terrorist attacks globally died in Pakistan. Source: https://www.visionofhumanity.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Global-Terrorism-Index-2026-Report.pdf
tweets.content:
Congratulations, Pakistan. You're Finally No. 1.

Not in education.
Not in the economy.
Not in innovation.

According to the Global Terrorism Index 2026, Pakistan is now ranked as the world's most terrorism affected country, overtaking Burkina Faso.

The report states that nearly 20% of all terrorism related deaths worldwide occurred in Pakistan. In other words, around one in every five people killed in terrorist attacks globally died in Pakistan.

Source:
visionofhumanity.org/wp-cont…
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border. @BSF_India @BSF_Rajasthan @Bikaner_Police @IgpBikaner @FaridkotPolice @FaridkotRange @sgnrpolice @ips_mridul #BikanerPolice #RajasthanPolice #DroneSmuggling #Pakistan #BorderSecurity #IllegalArms #NationalSecurity #IndiaPakistanBorder #CrimeNews #BreakingNews #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil
tweets.content:
पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले

In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border.
@BSF_India @BSF_Rajasthan @Bikaner_Police @IgpBikaner @FaridkotPolice @FaridkotRange @sgnrpolice @ips_mridul
#BikanerPolice #RajasthanPolice #DroneSmuggling #Pakistan #BorderSecurity #IllegalArms #NationalSecurity #IndiaPakistanBorder #CrimeNews #BreakingNews #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil

tweets.title: "Pakistan's problem is not just KASHMIR. It is India's RISE": Christina Fair If Kashmir was resolved tomorrow, would Pakistan stop confronting India? According to Christina Fair, the answer is a big NO. Fair highlights Pakistan's own military narrative: "Even after losing the 1971 war and losing half of the country, Pakistan claims it was not defeated and remains the only power capable of challenging India's rise in South Asia." Fair argues that Kashmir is only one part of a much larger rivalry. According to her analysis, the core objective is to challenge India's growing power and prevent its rise as a dominant force in South Asia Courtesy: @PeerzadaMoid
tweets.content:
"Pakistan's problem is not just KASHMIR. It is India's RISE": Christina Fair

If Kashmir was resolved tomorrow, would Pakistan stop confronting India? According to Christina Fair, the answer is a big NO.

Fair highlights Pakistan's own military narrative: "Even after losing the 1971 war and losing half of the country, Pakistan claims it was not defeated and remains the only power capable of challenging India's rise in South Asia."

Fair argues that Kashmir is only one part of a much larger rivalry. According to her analysis, the core objective is to challenge India's growing power and prevent its rise as a dominant force in South Asia

Courtesy: @PeerzadaMoid


Video
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: 🚨 Breaking News A report circulating from Pakistan claims that on July 8, a Pakistani Army convoy traveling from Balochistan carrying 250 soldiers was ambushed, allegedly resulting in 106 casualties, with 14 additional casualties reported later. The report further alleges that these casualty figures were withheld from the public and media, and were never officially disclosed as face saving. Source: Claims made by Shehbaz Gill. These claims have not been independently verified, and there has been no official confirmation of these figures.
tweets.content:
🚨 Breaking News

A report circulating from Pakistan claims that on July 8, a Pakistani Army convoy traveling from Balochistan carrying 250 soldiers was ambushed, allegedly resulting in 106 casualties, with 14 additional casualties reported later.

The report further alleges that these casualty figures were withheld from the public and media, and were never officially disclosed as face saving.

Source: Claims made by Shehbaz Gill. These claims have not been independently verified, and there has been no official confirmation of these figures.

tweets.author: @Drishti_K47
